english: dpañ byéd-pa (dpang byed pa) to act as a mediator and witness of the confirmation of the peace by oath Glr.; *mná zá-ba* (*mna' za ba*) C. to swear falsely, to commit perjury.

Dzl. and elsewh., Cs.: a son's or grand-son's wife, a daughter-in-law; but the word is also used for the daughter-in-law 'in spe', i.e. for the bride of the son, who is usually selected by the parents and lives with these for one or two years before being married; so also bridegroom and son-in-law are nearly synonymous; v. bág-ma (bag ma) and mág-pa (mag pa); cf. also the Hebrew חָתָן and כַּלָּה.

to suffer, to be tormented, B., C., sdug-bsñál púñ-pos (sdug bsngal phung pos) under a mountain of misery Glr.; nyes-méd γtsó-bo rgyál-poi ǰígs-pas mnar (nyes med gtso bo rgyal po'i 'jigs pas mnar) the innocent lords had to suffer in consequence of the king's fears Pth.; lás-kyis mnár-ba (las kyis mnar ba) to suffer in consequence of former actions, to be damned; lás-kyis mnár-bai brág-srin-mo žig (las kyis mnar ba'i brag srin mo zhig) a Srinmo in the state of damnation; rañ-nyíd mnar-sdañ (?) byed (rang nyid mnar sdang (?) byed) you make yourselves suffer the torments of damnation Mil.

resp. for γnyíd (gnyid), sleep, mnál-du péb-pa (mnal du pheb pa) or gró-ba ('gro ba) to fall asleep, mnál-ba (mnal ba) to sleep, mnál-γzim-pa (mnal gzim pa) id.; mnal sád-pa (mnal sad pa) to awake Mil.; mnal-láb (mnal lab) the talking in one's sleep; mnál-lam (mnal lam) dream Glr.

1. to think, fancy, imagine, de ñá-la zér-ba yin mnós-nas (de nga la zer ba yin mnos nas) thinking it had been said to him. — 2. to think upon, to consider, sña bsam pýi mno méd-par (snga bsam phyi mno med par) neither considering before hand, nor thinking of the consequences; bsam-mnó ytóñ-ba (bsam mno gtong ba) id., Mil. (cf. bsam-bló (bsam blo)).
contentment Cs.; zas-mnóg (zas mnog) Lex. w.e.; Sch.: moderate fare, frugal diet; mnog-čúñ (mnog chung) insignificant, trifling, v. nañs (nangs).
Cs. = mnal-grib (mnal grib); mnol-rig (mnol rig) weak intellect, want of quick perception Sch.
1. v. nód-pa (nod pa). — 2. v. mnó-ba (mno ba).

1. resp. snyan (snyan), col. *nám-čog* (*nam cog*), or ǎm-čog* (*am cog*), (Pur., Bal. *rna* (*rna*), *sna* (*sna*)), the ear, séms-čan o̥n-pa-dag rná-bas sgrá-rnams tos (sems can 'on pa dag rna bas sgra rnams thos) the deaf hear; rná-bai mé-loñ (rna ba'i me long) the drum or tympanum of the ear Cs.; rná-bai dgá-ston (rna ba'i dga' ston) a treat for the ears Glr.; rná-bai dbáñ-po γtod (rna ba'i dbang po gtod) lend me your ear, listen to me Mil.; ñed rná-ba mi sun (nged rna ba mi sun) I am not tired of hearing Mil.; rnar snyán-pa (rnar snyan pa) pleasant to the ear, tickling the ear Stg.; rná-ba d̥úd-pa (rna ba 'dud pa) v. d̥úd-pa ('dud pa); rná-ba byá-ba (rna ba bya ba), byó-ba (byo ba), blág-pa (blag pa) Sch., to listen; rná-ba ná-ba (rna ba na ba) disease of the ear, ear-ache; rná-ba u̥r-ba (rna ba 'ur ba) Med. a tingling, humming, or buzzing in the ears; rná-ba sra (rna ba sra) hard or dull of hearing Sch. — 2. v. rñá-ba (rnga ba). Comp. rna-kór (rna skor) ear-ring Sch. — *na-kyág* (*na kyag*) W. ear-wax, cerumen. — rna-kúñ (rna khung) ear-hole, c̥i-bai rná-kuñ-du ('chi ba'i rna khung du) (or rná-bar (rna bar), or rnar (rnar)) brǰód-pa (brjod pa) to cry into a dying man's ear. — rna-kěbs (rna khebs) that part of a helmet which protects the ear Sch. — rna-gyán (rna rgyan) ornament worn in the ears, e.g. mé-tog-gi (me tog gi) Stg.; rna-čá (rna cha) id., γsér-gyi (gser gyi) Mil. — rná-mčog (rna mchog) col. 1. = rná-ba (rna ba). 2. the pan of a fire-lock. — rna-ltág (rna ltag) the back-part of the ear Cs. — rná-teg-čan (rna theg can), bzód-pa sgóm-pai rná-teg-čan (bzod pa sgom pa'i rna theg can) one that is able to listen to all that (stuff) with patience Mil. — rna-γdúb (rna gdub) ear-ring Cs. — rna-mdá yzér-ba (rna mda' gzer ba) C. the piercing of the ear with an arrow, a chinese punishment. — rna-spág (rna spag) (sic), or -spábs (-spabs) ear-wax Sch. — rna-rál (rna ral) an ear torn by pendants. — rna-lúñ (rna lung) Cs. the ear or handle of a vessel. — rna-šál (rna shal) Med. ear-lap, tip of the ear. — rna(-pa)-γšóg (rna(-pa)-gshog) Lex. and Lt., perh. = sna-γšóg (sna gshog). — rna-slán (rna slan) (*nas-lán* (*nas lan*)) a fur-cover for the ears, worn by Tibetan ladies.

matter, pus, suppuration, rnag smín-pa (rnag smin pa) pus grown ripe Cs.; d̥rén-pa ('dren pa) Sch.: 'to draw out the pus'; (I only met with rnag sná-d̥ren-pa (rnag sna 'dren pa) S.g., which can hardly have this signification); rnag-rdól-ba (rnag rdol ba) discharge of matter; rnag-rtól-ba (rnag rtol ba) prob. causing such a discharge by a puncture; rnag d̥zág-pa (rnag 'dzag pa) the dropping or running of pus
